feedback.text: Many online games, such as Battlefield Play4Free or Need for Speed World, were free, but required payments for allowances such as weapons or cars. After several years, the servers of these games were shut down, resulting in hundreds of euros being spent on them. The lack of self-hosting of servers prevents further game, e.g. on LAN party. In addition, payment processors such as VISA and Mastercard are pushing digital platforms to remove legal NSFW content, threatening to withdraw services, which may lead to the loss of purchased content. In addition, the imposition by third-party platforms such as YouTube of age verification as in the current UK regulations raises concerns about restrictions on freedom of expression and access to content. European consumers are losing access to the games and services they have invested in. Regulations are needed to protect games and digital services after support, against uncontrolled financial censorship and excessive verification restrictions, so that purchased products remain in place for as long as they are in the user’s possession.
